title: PlayerHousing
description: Housing system for Minecraft that allows players to have their very own customizable world.
body:
# PlayerHousing  
This plugin is designed to allow players to have as much control over their world as possible. What is the point in this plugin? well, lets first talk about the big issue with creative server which is lag machines. with using multiple worlds, lag machines are nothing more that a world crasher and wont effect the the hub world or any other world for that matter.

## Note
PlayerHousing was originally coded for Folia and was ported to other server software so performance may very between software.

# Housing API
You can access the (ugly) api by calling the api instance from "PlayerHousing.getAPI()".
You can also check out the source code listed on [Github](https://github.com/wildmaster84/PlayerHousing) if you just wanna take a look.

# Features
- Fully GUI driven
- World Presets
- GameRules Manager
- Difficulty Manager
- Gamemode Manager
- Built-in Player Manager
- Player Whitelists
- Public and Private Worlds
- World Manager
- Custom Addon Support

# Installation
- Place the plugin into the plugins folder and cycle the server
- Place a world folder into "/plugins/PlayerHosuing/presets" and modify the config to reflect the presets

# Commands
- /visit | Opens world menu
- /visit <player> | Joins a players world
- /housing | Opens the housing menu

# Extra
This plugin has been in development for the past year and a half to ensure players wont get soft-locked or something internal wont break. I am aware that some things need to be improved, but i worked as hard on this plugin as i could to ensure it does not effect performance whatsoever. If you have any issues or feature request them feel free to open a ticket on github!
